turns.text: Did the record show whether he ran on a platform of doing this which he says he was trying to do it?

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: I think the Attorney General made that statement. There isn't any question and after all, it's almost a matter of public record of which I believe you could take judicial notice if it's not in there. In so many words, he ran independently. He had no political party. There isn't any statement there that he said the judges were an association of armed robbers. He says, “I have no organization. My opponents are the association of armed robbers.” He wasn't talking about the judges. That's -- a string that that language won't take --

turns.name: POTTER STEWART
turns.text: Who was he talking about?

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: Pardon?

turns.name: POTTER STEWART
turns.text: Who was he talking about?

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: The political organizations which had opposed him in his elect. I have no organization. I have no -- he says that. That's expressed on opponent, I think. My opponents are the association of armed robbers, meaning the professional political organizations which had opposed him in his election. He was elected --

turns.name: TOM CLARK
turns.text: Well I --

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: Pardon?

turns.name: TOM CLARK
turns.text: He had the --

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: What maybe the opin --?

turns.name: TOM CLARK
turns.text: Well I -- are the association of armed robbers.

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: I think -- well, I didn't have it performed but here the sentence before that says, “I have no organization --

turns.name: TOM CLARK
turns.text: It turned to them.

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: That's -- and that follows right with it. “I have no organization, the other officials are the association of armed robbers.”

turns.name: HUGO BLACK
turns.text: Do you think that statement was what the people used they called to proceed but they won't admit it at least that they were bad people and he was a good man.

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: Well, I don't think there's any question you could ask and he would answer that question affirmatively.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Mr. Deutsch, -- Mr. Deutsch, how long was he in office before this judge attacked him for --

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: For, well --

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: -- for suggesting the calendar?

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: He took office in May. This controversy started three or four months later, I think. I suppose I can get it. The statement which is the subject of this -- once the subject of this prosecution was made on November 2nd, published on November 2nd as I recall it. Now, there had been a building up of the controversy and statements back and forth when the retired judge made his statement charging appellant with responsibility for the backlog of these cases, that wasn't the first thing that was said on this subject that had been going back and forth.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Very well.

turns.name: POTTER STEWART
turns.text: Do you think it's a -- is there any difference whether this statement, they maybe run and attacked upon these judges in their official rather than their personal character?

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: Well, I think the judgment must go with me in either event but I do think it makes that difference because it brings it under the scope of what we have called sedition in the last 175 or 100 maybe year.

turns.name: POTTER STEWART
turns.text: You say that it attacked upon them solely and exclusively in their official capacity?

turns.name: EBERHARD P. DEUTSCH
turns.text: Would -- would make it sedition in the -- shall I say appropriate sense. I don't know whether it was an offense for him to make the statement as to what he thought this Court would ultimately do in this case. I certainly had my very strong views and I ask you to support it.

turns.name: JACK P. F. GREMILLION
turns.text: Mr. Chief Justice, may I ask you one thing?

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes, General.

turns.name: JACK P. F. GREMILLION
turns.text: What he -- what Mr. Deutsch is just saying about the organizations, that's his interpretation of it but he, the District Attorney, never did explained that and there's nothing in the record to show that he was talking about a political organization. So I don't know what he was talking about but I do know that he was in a controversy with these judges and that's when -- that was a subject matter of his speech at that particular auditorium.

turns.name: EARL WARREN
turns.text: Yes, very well.
